era_context: Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin; Old Kingdom Egypt (6th Dynasty) in decline; Harappan civilization in mature phase; Xia Dynasty traditionally dated to this era. The 4.2 kya climate event is ongoing, with paleoclimatic impacts across Eurasia and Africa.

events.title: Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin; military campaigns and consolidation
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues. The Akkadian Empire maintains control over Mesopotamia and extends influence into the Levant and surrounding regions. Military campaigns are ongoing to suppress rebellions and defend against external pressure. The empire's administrative apparatus continues to function, as evidenced by surviving texts and inscriptions.
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but precise year-by-year chronology is uncertain. The year -2204 is assigned by convention.

events.title: Egyptian 6th Dynasty; decline of Old Kingdom authority
events.description: The 6th Dynasty of Egypt continues its decline. Central authority weakens further. Provincial governors accumulate power and resources. The Nile's inundation remains erratic. The state's ability to organize large-scale projects has effectively ceased. Egypt is moving toward the First Intermediate Period.

sources.name: Egyptian king lists and tomb inscriptions
Paleoclimatic records indicating reduced Nile inundation

events.certainty_note: The 6th Dynasty's decline is well-documented, but precise dating remains uncertain.

events.title: Harappan civilization in mature phase; continued urban flourishing
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature phase. Urban centers continue to flourish. Trade networks remain active. Standardized weights and measures indicate sophisticated economic administration. No major disruptions are evident at this time.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Indus Valley sites

events.certainty_note: The Harappan civilization's continued flourishing is well-attested archaeologically.
cross_references: Part of broader Harappan mature phase

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The year -2204 is not distinctly documented in historical sources. Most events of this period are dated to broader ranges. The surviving sources are heavily weighted toward Mesopotamia and Egypt. Other regions are underrepresented or absent from the written record. The four years -2204 to -2201 are largely indistinguishable in the historical record; they represent a period of gradual decline and stress rather than discrete, datable events.

graph_edges.note: Naram-Sin's reign continues toward the Akkadian collapse
